config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru dari perlindungan hak pribadi lah masyarakat yang sehat bisa dibangun. Saya paham bahwa dalam keadaan tertentu kepentingan umum bisa membatasi kebebasan individu, tetapi itu harus sangat ketat, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menindas kelompok kecil atau suara yang tidak populer. Di Indonesia, saya melihat bahaya besar kalau “kepentingan সমাজ” dipakai terlalu longgar, karena sering kali yang kuat bisa mengatasnamakan mayoritas untuk menekan yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach by governments, majorities, or institutions. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public safety, basic order, and preventing direct harm can justify limits, but those limits need to be narrow and accountable. When people say “society” as if it were one unified will, I get skeptical, because in practice it usually means the loudest or most powerful group. A healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first and then limiting them only when there is a clear, specific, and defensible reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu tidak boleh diposisikan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat secara umum, karena di Indonesia kata “masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan mayoritas atau elite terhadap orang yang lemah. Saya setuju ada kondisi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik, bencana, atau pencegahan kerugian nyata yang bisa membatasi hak individu, tetapi itu harus jelas dasar hukumnya, sempit, dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan bersama justru mudah berubah menjadi alat untuk menekan kebebasan beragama, berpendapat, atau hidup berbeda. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak individu sambil tetap menjaga kepentingan umum secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States “society first” has too often been a cover for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; they can be restricted for real public safety, but only with narrow rules, due process, and clear evidence of harm. What I reject is the vague idea that society has a standing priority over the individual, because that usually ends up meaning the strongest group gets its way. A free society should protect individual rights as the default and justify any limits case by case.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan orang yang minoritas, berbeda, atau tidak punya kuasa. Bagi saya, hak individu harus jadi titik awal, lalu boleh dibatasi hanya kalau ada bahaya nyata, dasar hukum yang jelas, dan pembatasannya benar-benar proporsional. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan kepentingan bersama, melainkan mayoritas atau elite yang memaksakan kehendak atas nama semua orang. Masyarakat yang adil justru kuat ketika ia melindungi hak individu, bukan ketika ia otomatis mengorbankannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as too broad, because in the United States “society first” has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. Society does matter, but it is not a single moral voice, and in practice the people claiming to speak for it are often the most powerful or the loudest. Individual rights should be the default, and any limits need to be narrow, evidence-based, and tied to real harm rather than vague appeals to the common good. A healthy society is not one that automatically outranks the individual; it is one that protects individual liberty while making careful exceptions when public safety truly requires them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
